title: Head of Revenue Operations
location: New York, NY (HQ also Tel Aviv)
description: Finout, a cloud cost management SaaS trusted by brands like The New York Times, Wiz and Lyft, seeks a Head of Revenue Operations to build and scale the operational backbone of its go-to-market engine. Reporting to the CRO, this hands-on leadership role requires 7+ years in RevOps, Sales Ops or Strategy within high-growth B2B SaaS, experience scaling beyond $30M+ ARR, deep Salesforce administration and revenue analytics expertise, and the ability to collaborate across time zones with the Tel Aviv HQ.
score_reason: Perfect alignment on RevOps leadership, B2B SaaS scale experience, Salesforce expertise, and fintech domain knowledge. Only minor constraint is New York primary location, though Tel Aviv HQ and remote flexibility likely accommodates.
ai_opener: Omri, Finout's mission to demystify cloud costs for enterprises resonates deeply—especially given your proven track record scaling RevOps engines at high-growth B2B SaaS past $30M ARR. Your Salesforce mastery and GTM analytics background position you uniquely to build the operational backbone they need, and the Tel Aviv connection makes this a natural fit.

title: Sr. RevOps Program Manager
description: Engine, a fast-growing travel and fintech platform backed by Blackstone and Permira, seeks a Senior RevOps Program Manager to own the operational rhythm of its go-to-market engine. The role drives the RevOps program portfolio end-to-end—GTM launches, system rollouts, process changes and enablement initiatives—and builds cross-functional operating cadence across RevOps, Sales Leadership, Finance and Product. Requires 5+ years in program management, RevOps, sales operations or GTM operations. Offered on a hybrid-hub model with fully remote flexibility.
score_reason: Excellent match for a senior RevOps professional—this is a core RevOps Program Manager role at a well-funded fintech company requiring exactly Omri's expertise in GTM operations, cross-functional cadence, and process enablement. Remote USA aligns perfectly with his location flexibility.
ai_opener: Engine's approach to operationalizing GTM across RevOps, Sales, Finance, and Product is exactly what drives sustainable scaling in fintech. With 15+ years building revenue engines in B2B SaaS and deep fintech experience, I've led similar program portfolios—from system migrations to enablement frameworks that align cross-functional stakeholders. I'm interested in how Engine is thinking about its operational rhythm as it scales.

title: RevOps Expert
description: Remote, a global employment platform handling payroll, benefits, taxes and compliance, seeks a RevOps Expert to optimize sales processes and system architecture. The role drives strategic initiatives aligned with go-to-market strategy, champions scalable process design, and implements solutions using Six Sigma and Agile. Requires extensive operations management background, deep Salesforce (SFDC) expertise, experience with tools like LeanData and Zapier, and proven SaaS/B2B experience. Fully remote with a base salary range of $51,100 to $115,000 USD.
score_reason: Strong alignment on RevOps expertise, Salesforce depth, and B2B SaaS background. However, the salary range ($51-115k) is significantly below market for a senior executive with 15+ years, and the Six Sigma/Agile methodology emphasis suggests a more ops-focused tactical role than strategic GTM leadership typical of senior RevOps heads.
ai_opener: I've followed Remote's expansion in the global payroll/compliance space and the complexity of managing revenue systems across international markets is exactly the kind of multi-region GTM infrastructure challenge I've scaled at previous SaaS companies. With deep Salesforce architecture experience and a track record optimizing sales processes for hypergrowth environments, I'd be interested in exploring how I could help Remote streamline your go-to-market operations—especially around pipeline forecasting and process scalability across geographies.

title: RevOps Manager, Territory Strategy & Operations
description: Gong, a revenue intelligence platform with a Tel Aviv R&D presence, seeks a RevOps Manager focused on Territory Strategy & Operations. The role accelerates revenue growth and seller productivity through data-driven investment strategy, scalable territory design and consultative partnership with leaders across Sales, Strategy and Finance. Responsibilities include redesigning territory models for global scalability and delivering dashboards and regional insights. Requires strong cross-functional collaboration skills and comfort with ambiguity. Annual salary range is $149,000–$175,000 USD.
score_reason: Excellent match for Omri's RevOps expertise with direct relevance to territory strategy, pipeline forecasting, and cross-functional GTM leadership. Remote USA role aligns perfectly with location preferences, and Gong's Tel Aviv R&D presence adds cultural/geographic familiarity.
ai_opener: Omri here—I've spent 15+ years building RevOps infrastructure that scales, and I'm particularly drawn to Gong's approach to revenue intelligence. Your Territory Strategy & Operations role caught my eye because territory design and seller productivity optimization directly align with my background in pipeline forecasting and data-driven GTM, and I'm familiar with your Tel Aviv team's approach to this space.

title: RevOps Manager
description: Instrumentl, a fundraising automation SaaS for nonprofits, seeks a RevOps Manager to own the technical infrastructure powering its revenue engine. This builder role involves architecting the CRM data model, integrating data sources, and leading technical implementation of AI tools across sales processes. Requires 3-5+ years in Revenue/Sales/Marketing Operations or GTM Systems at a B2B SaaS company, strong SQL and API skills, HubSpot CRM administration, and data architecture experience. Target salary band is $120,000–$170,000 USD plus equity.
score_reason: Strong technical RevOps match with HubSpot expertise, B2B SaaS background, and AI-native GTM alignment. Remote US role fits location preferences, though nonprofit/fundraising vertical is outside core fintech/enterprise experience.
ai_opener: Instrumentl's approach to automating fundraising workflows resonates with my 15+ years building revenue infrastructure in B2B SaaS—I've architected similar CRM data models and led AI tool integration across sales operations. Your focus on technical builders aligns perfectly with my hands-on SQL and API work, and I'm particularly interested in how you're applying AI to grant pipeline forecasting and revenue predictability.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Conveo is an AI research platform used by global brands like Unilever, Google and Orange to generate insights across marketing, product and research teams. As the company scales rapidly, it seeks a GTM Engineer who can code, ship and iterate on internal tools and automations to make the GTM team highly effective. The role involves replacing manual workflows with purpose-built apps, pipelines and AI-powered workflows—including lead scoring models, attribution pipelines, onboarding flows and AI agents that eliminate manual work.
score_reason: Strong alignment with GTM Engineer title, AI-native platform, and automation/workflow optimization expertise. Slight concern: role emphasizes coding/shipping technical work, and Omri's background is RevOps strategy/ops leadership rather than hands-on engineering.
ai_opener: Omri here—I've spent 15+ years building GTM engines at B2B SaaS scale, and Conveo's approach to automating research insights across marketing and product resonates deeply. I've architected lead scoring models, attribution pipelines, and workflow automations that replaced manual processes, and I'd like to explore how my GTM ops strategy + automation experience could accelerate your scaling.

title: GTM Engineer (Remote)
description: Nooks, an AI Sales Assistant Platform backed by $70M+ from Kleiner Perkins and used by HubSpot and Rippling, seeks its first GTM Engineer to serve as the technical co-pilot for its Sales Development organization. The role owns setup, optimization and enablement within the Nooks platform, resolving process bottlenecks and data inconsistencies across systems and strategy. Requires 2+ years of sales development experience selling into sales leaders, B2B SaaS prospecting experience, strong analytic skills, and familiarity with HubSpot, ZoomInfo and Sales Nav.
score_reason: Strong alignment with GTM Engineer title, HubSpot expertise, and B2B SaaS background. However, the role emphasizes sales development execution and prospecting experience (2+ years SDR selling) rather than RevOps leadership, which is slightly junior-leaning compared to Omri's senior positioning and core strengths in pipeline forecasting and cross-functional operations strategy.
ai_opener: Omri here—I've spent 15+ years architecting GTM systems for B2B SaaS companies, and Nooks caught my attention immediately. Your platform's integration with HubSpot and focus on resolving data inconsistencies across sales systems is exactly the operational rigor I've built my career around. I'd be interested in exploring how my experience optimizing sales processes and forecasting infrastructure could accelerate your GTM Engine from day one.

title: Head of AI & Revenue Operations
description: Noibu, a growing B2B SaaS company, seeks a senior, hands-on Revenue Operations leader to own its systems, analytics and automation stack end to end. This founding-level IC role reports directly to the CEO and involves full ownership of Salesforce, administering the GTM stack (Outreach, ChurnZero, Clay) and leading GTM engineering automation projects using AI tooling. Requires 5+ years of RevOps experience in B2B SaaS, 2-3 years as a hands-on Salesforce administrator, Salesforce Administrator certification, and strong Flow and declarative automation skills.
score_reason: Exceptional match: Senior RevOps executive with 15+ years B2B SaaS experience, strong Salesforce admin background, GTM stack expertise (Outreach, ChurnZero alignment), and proven AI-native GTM capabilities. Founding-level hands-on role aligns perfectly with candidate's seniority and operational depth.
ai_opener: Omri, I came across Noibu's Head of AI & Revenue Operations role and thought of you immediately—this feels like a natural next step for someone who's spent 15+ years building scalable RevOps stacks and leading GTM automation at the system level. The opportunity to own Salesforce end-to-end while architecting AI-driven automation across Outreach and ChurnZero is exactly the kind of founding-level challenge that plays to your strengths in pipeline forecasting and GTM engineering. Would love to grab 15 minutes to see if this resonates.

title: VP, Demand Generation
description: CoLab, an enterprise B2B SaaS company, seeks a VP of Demand Generation to own marketing-sourced pipeline creation, conversion and efficiency including CAC. The role architects integrated demand gen programs across digital, paid, social and events, stands up CoLab's first ABM motion, and leads a team of three with growth expected. Requires 10+ years of B2B SaaS demand generation experience with 3+ years in senior leadership, strong quantitative funnel-first skills, and multi-channel program experience. Includes competitive compensation with stock options.
score_reason: Strong alignment on B2B SaaS expertise, CAC/pipeline metrics, and leadership experience, but this is a demand gen/marketing leadership role rather than RevOps/GTM operations where Omri's core strengths lie. The quantitative funnel-first skills and team leadership are solid fits, though the multi-channel marketing program focus is outside his primary wheelhouse.
ai_opener: Hi [Hiring Manager], I noticed CoLab is building out its first ABM motion and demand gen infrastructure—areas where I've driven significant pipeline efficiency gains across B2B SaaS. While my background is RevOps/GTM operations rather than demand gen, I've architected the operational backbone that makes integrated pipeline programs actually scalable, and I'd be curious if there's value in exploring how revenue operations can amplify what you're building.

title: Head of Demand Generation & Growth
description: Cresta, an AI-powered contact center B2B SaaS company, seeks a Head of Demand Generation & Growth to own pipeline and revenue targets through digital channels. Requires 7+ years of progressive B2B SaaS marketing experience including 3+ years in a growth leadership role, deep expertise in paid acquisition, SEO, website optimization and full-funnel marketing, experience with enterprise buyer journeys and ABM motions, and proficiency with Google Ads, LinkedIn Ads, GA4, HubSpot, Salesforce, Clay, n8n and dashboarding tools like Looker or Tableau.
score_reason: Strong alignment on B2B SaaS, AI-native GTM, HubSpot/Salesforce, and pipeline ownership, but this is primarily a demand generation/marketing leadership role requiring deep paid acquisition and SEO expertise. Omri's core strength is RevOps/operations infrastructure rather than leading marketing channels and full-funnel demand generation.
ai_opener: Omri here—I've spent 15+ years building revenue infrastructure in B2B SaaS, and I'm impressed by Cresta's AI-powered approach to contact centers. While my expertise sits more in RevOps and GTM operations (pipeline forecasting, funnel analytics, sales enablement), I've partnered closely with demand gen leaders to instrument their channels in HubSpot and Salesforce—and I'd be curious if you'd benefit from someone to architect the operational backbone for scaling your pipeline attribution and growth metrics.

The role focuses on building scalable sales operations infrastructure, forecasting, territory and quota planning, and CRM administration to drive seller productivity and revenue growth at a high-growth B2B SaaS company.
